title: 60-year-old man with progressive difficulty swallowing
candidateInstructions: You are an FY2 doctor in the GP surgery. Mr Robert Caldwell, a 60-year-old retired publican, presents with a 4-month history of difficulty swallowing. Please take a focused history and determine the most likely diagnosis.
examinerNotes: Progressive dysphagia solids > liquids in an elderly man with significant alcohol and smoking history — oesophageal carcinoma until proven otherwise. Must elicit progressive nature, weight loss, GORD history. Needs urgent 2-week wait endoscopy referral.

patient.name: Robert Caldwell
patient.presentingComplaint: Difficulty swallowing — food seems to get stuck
patient.reasonForAttendance: I keep choking on my food. Solid food sticks. Starting to worry me.

hpi.site: Feels food sticking behind the breastbone
hpi.onset: Gradual, started 4 months ago with occasional difficulty with bread and meat
hpi.character: Food sticking, requiring water to wash it down
hpi.timing: Progressive — now occurring with every meal
hpi.relievingFactors: Soft foods and liquids — started living on soups
hpi.severity: Now struggling with soft foods too, can only swallow liquids without difficulty
hpi.previousEpisodes: History of GORD for 15 years, on antacids

alcohol.pattern: Heavy drinker — worked in pub trade for 30 years, now drinks 5 units daily

socialHistory.occupation: Retired publican — worked in pubs for 30 years

systemsReview.gastrointestinal: Dysphagia, regurgitation, weight loss, no haematemesis, no melaena

examination.abdominal: No hepatomegaly, left supraclavicular lymph node palpable

investigations.name: Upper GI Endoscopy (OGD)
investigations.result: Irregular, ulcerated, friable mass at 30-35cm from incisors in the mid-oesophagus. Biopsies taken. Lumen significantly narrowed.
investigations.interpretation: Mid-oesophageal carcinoma. Biopsy pending — likely squamous cell carcinoma given risk factors. Urgent staging CT required.

investigations.name: Chest X-ray
investigations.result: Widening of mediastinum in mid-zone. No pulmonary metastases. Left hilar enlargement.
investigations.interpretation: Mediastinal widening and hilar enlargement — raises concern for mediastinal extension of oesophageal tumour.

patient.emotionalState: Stoic but clearly worried. Has been avoiding seeking help. Knows something is wrong but scared to hear it.
patient.communicationStyle: Brief answers. Minimises problems. Direct when asked specific questions.
hiddenConcerns: I know this sounds bad — my wife thinks it might be cancer
I've been putting off coming for months
The hoarse voice started recently and really scared me
doNotVolunteer: Hoarse voice — only if asked about changes to voice
Weight loss amount — will say 'a bit' unless asked how much
GORD history — only if asked about heartburn or reflux
Alcohol units per week — will underestimate unless pressed specifically

dataGathering.criterion: Characterises dysphagia — solids vs liquids, progressive
dataGathering.detailedExpectation: Establishes whether for solids first (mechanical obstruction) and progression
Key risk factors for oesophageal SCC
Risk factor for adenocarcinoma — Barrett's oesophagus
Recognises need for urgent upper GI endoscopy

clinicalManagement.criterion: Identifies oesophageal cancer as primary concern

clinicalManagement.detailedExpectation: Refers urgently to gastroenterology or upper GI surgery

interpersonalSkills.criterion: Sensitively raises concern about serious diagnosis
interpersonalSkills.detailedExpectation: Raises cancer possibility without certainty, compassionately

references.source: NICE NG12: Suspected cancer — recognition and referral

learningObjectives: Identify risk factors for oesophageal carcinoma
Recognise red flag symptoms requiring 2-week wait referral
